unit.name: Vision Transformer refresher and modern transformer tweaks
unit.description: Recaps how images are processed with ViTs, then surveys common transformer modifications used to stabilize training and increase capacity.
unit.summary: The lecture revisits vision transformers by explaining that an image is split into patches, each patch is linearly projected into a D-dimensional token, and positional embeddings are added because patching discards 2D location information [ts=210s] [ts=245s] [ts=278s]. For classification, the instructor contrasts two readout strategies: a learned class token whose output becomes class probabilities, or pooling all output patch tokens before a final projection; unlike autoregressive language models, ViTs do not need masking because all image patches can attend to each other [ts=354s] [ts=440s] [ts=464s]. The segment then summarizes practical transformer tweaks: pre-norm moves normalization inside residual paths to preserve an identity route, RMSNorm is a simpler normalization that empirically stabilizes training, SwiGLU replaces the plain MLP with a gated nonlinearity, and mixture-of-experts routes each token to A of E experts to increase parameters with a smaller compute increase [ts=587s] [ts=609s] [ts=653s] [ts=725s].

key_points.text: A ViT converts an image into a sequence by splitting it into patches.
Each image patch is reshaped and linearly projected into a D-dimensional token.
Positional embeddings are added because patching loses the image's 2D position information.
A learned class token can be appended so its output becomes the class-probability vector.
An alternative ViT classifier pools all output tokens and projects the pooled vector to class scores.
Pre-norm moves normalization before self-attention and the MLP so the residual path can preserve identity behavior.
RMSNorm normalizes by root-mean-square magnitude instead of using the mean and is presented as more stable in training.
Mixture-of-experts routes each token to A active experts out of E total experts, increasing parameters faster than compute.

kp_catalog.name: ViT patch tokenization and positional encoding
kp_catalog.description: Representing an image as a sequence of patch tokens, then restoring spatial order with positional embeddings before transformer processing.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because viT patch tokenization and positional encoding formalizes how representing an image as a sequence of patch tokens, then restoring spatial order with positional embeddings before transformer processing, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.

kp_catalog.name: ViT readout strategies for classification
kp_catalog.description: Two common ways to turn transformer outputs into image classes: a learned class token or pooling all output tokens before classification.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because viT readout strategies for classification clarifies two common ways to turn transformer outputs into image classes: a learned class token or pooling all output tokens before classification, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Transformer stability and capacity tweaks
kp_catalog.description: Pre-norm, RMSNorm, SwiGLU, and mixture-of-experts are practical modifications that stabilize training or increase model capacity.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because transformer stability and capacity tweaks clarifies pre-norm, RMSNorm, SwiGLU, and mixture-of-experts are practical modifications that stabilize training or increase model capacity, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
